questions.question: Explain the safety and liveness requirements in blockchain consensus.
questions.modelAnswer: Safety ensures that honest nodes never commit conflicting blocks at the same height, preventing double-spending and maintaining ledger consistency. Liveness guarantees that valid transactions are eventually processed, ensuring the system remains usable. Under network partitions, a trade-off exists: prioritizing safety may cause halting, while prioritizing liveness may cause forks. Both properties are essential - without safety, the ledger is unreliable; without liveness, the system is unusable.

questions.question: Describe the blockchain trilemma and how different blockchains address it.
keyPoints: Define three properties: Decentralization, Security, Scalability
Explain why only 2 of 3 can be optimized

questions.question: What is Sybil resistance and how do PoW and PoS achieve it?
keyPoints: PoW: Requires computational resources per identity
PoS: Requires economic stake per vote

questions.question: Compare the fault tolerance requirements of CFT and BFT systems with examples.
keyPoints: CFT: 2f+1 nodes for f crash faults
BFT: 3f+1 nodes for f Byzantine faults
